evals.name: slice-dr-into-approved-epics
evals.prompt: Using the existing DR/SRS, linked PRD, and completed Use Cases, propose several cohesive Jira Epics that can each be completed in a few two-week sprints. Group related Use Cases by outcome, show the proposed boundaries and traceability, and ask me to approve the grouping before drafting Jira items. Do not call Jira yet.
evals.expected_output: A reviewable Epic proposal grouped by cohesive outcome and bounded to a few sprints, with source traceability and an explicit approval gate before Jira mutation.
expectations: Proposes multiple Epic slices when one DR/SRS contains enough scope for more than one few-sprint outcome.
Groups Use Cases by cohesive outcome and keeps each Epic boundary explicit.
Shows traceability from each Epic to the parent DR/SRS, PRD/JTBD, and included UC-XX IDs.
Asks for explicit approval before drafting or mutating Jira items.

evals.name: write-actor-stories-and-technical-tasks
evals.prompt: After the Epic grouping is approved, create the Jira backlog projection for UC-01 and UC-02. UC-01 has primary actor "Cittadino pagatore" and UC-02 has primary actor "Operatore di assistenza". Write actor-facing Stories using exactly "As [Actor], I want to [action], so that [Gain]". Keep each Story deliverable in one two-week sprint. Put repository setup and API contract wiring into technical Tasks, and link every item to its Epic and source UC/AC IDs.
evals.expected_output: A Jira-ready draft containing canonical actor-facing Stories, sprint-sized scope, technical Tasks for enabling work, and complete hierarchy/traceability.
expectations: Uses the exact actor names supplied by the Use Cases.
Uses the canonical As/ I want/ so that Story structure.
Keeps Stories and Tasks scoped to one two-week sprint or flags work that must be split.
Represents repository setup and API contract wiring as Tasks rather than actor-facing Stories.
Links each item to its Epic and one or more source UC-XX or AC-XX IDs.

evals.name: block-incomplete-readiness
evals.prompt: Generate Jira backlog items from a DR/SRS whose PRD has no confirmed owner or KPI, the Use Case has no binary acceptance checks, and RFC-07 is accepted but not propagated into the DR/SRS. Explain what is blocked and identify the gaps. Do not invent values and do not create Jira items.
evals.expected_output: The skill blocks backlog creation, reports each readiness gap and its impact, and does not infer requirements or call Jira.
expectations: Blocks or pauses Jira creation because Definition of Ready evidence is incomplete.
Reports the missing PRD owner and KPI, missing binary acceptance checks, and unpropagated RFC decision.
Does not invent owners, KPI targets, acceptance criteria, or implementation behavior.
Does not create Jira items while material blockers remain unresolved.

evals.name: synchronize-existing-backlog
evals.prompt: Synchronize the existing Jira projection after UC-02 gains a confirmed retry flow. Preserve the existing Epic, UC-02 link, and unchanged AC-UC-02-* identifiers. Add only the new retry acceptance traceability, avoid duplicate issues, show a proposed diff, and wait for explicit confirmation before updating Jira.
evals.expected_output: A non-destructive synchronization proposal that matches existing issues by stable source identifiers, preserves unchanged data, and requires confirmation before updating.
expectations: Matches existing Jira items using stable source links or IDs and does not create duplicates.
Preserves the existing Epic, UC-02 identity, and unchanged acceptance-check IDs.
Adds only the new retry behavior and its new traceability.
Shows a human-readable proposed diff and asks for explicit confirmation before mutation.

evals.name: require-project-context
evals.prompt: Create the Jira projection from a Use Case, but do not provide a Jira project key or enough information to identify the target board. Ask only for the missing Jira context and stop without inventing a project or creating issues.
evals.expected_output: The skill requests the missing Jira project context and stops without making Jira changes.
expectations: Requests the Jira project key or unambiguous target board.
Does not guess a project, issue type mapping, or required custom field.
Does not create or update Jira items before the missing context is supplied.
